experimental_design: HMET1 PylRS/tRNAPyl pairs function in the same heterologous H. volcanii cell to incorporate distinct ncAAs into a single polypeptide programmed by different stop codons.
demonstrates that the HMET1 PylRS/tRNAPyl pairs function in the same heterologous H. volcanii cell to incorporate distinct ncAAs into a single polypeptide programmed by different stop codons.
number_of_fractions: No mention of fractionation in the text.
number_of_biological_replicates: we discovered two novel PylRS/tRNAPyl pairs
number_of_samples: we discovered two novel PylRS/tRNAPyl pairs
